calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Continued To Jul-08-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Action From Limited To Unlimited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Apr-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Juana L. Hernandez's Motion To Reclassify Action From Limited To Unlimited Jurisdiction is GRANTED.

Plaintiff's motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 403.040(a) fails because "the time allowed for [Plaintiff] to amend the initial pleading" has expired. Indeed, Plaintiff amended her initial pleading long ago-in July 2025-and Defendant answered long ago---in November 2025. The time to amend the "initial pleading" has certainly passed.

The court construes Plaintiff's request, which is unopposed, as one made under Code of Civil Procedure section 403.040(b) and, as such, grants it.

This action shall be reclassified upon Plaintiff's payment of the reclassification fee. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 403.040(d) and 4034.060.)

Prior to the hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Oct-02-2025 Continued To Dec-31-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Nils Rosenquest, Esq. 415-292-0980 Rosenquest & Associates for Plaintiff in limited scope. (In-person)
Aaron Hayes, Esq. 415-275-3541 O Hagne Meyer for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court reminds Mr. Rosenquest re rule 3.36. Plaintiff's counsel Mr. Hayes submits. Mr. Rosenquest contests and presents arguments on granting leave to amend on the second cause of action. Mr. Hayes submits. The court comments. Mr. Rosenquest informs the court he will be out of the country with no internet access and be given until 10/17/25 if the court changes its mind and grants leave to amend.

DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE REGIONAL CENTER DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Golden Gate Regional Center's demurrer to Plaintiff Juana L. Hernandez's First Amended Complaint.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, an order on demurrer is signed. (see scanned order for details)***
